title: 501. What does the term 'Endangered Species' mean?
hint: It refers to species facing a high risk of disappearing from the wild.
summary: Endangered species face a very high risk of extinction in the near future.

options.title: Species facing a very high risk of extinction

title: 502. Which organization publishes the Red List of Threatened Species?
hint: Its full form begins with International Union...
summary: The IUCN maintains the global Red List.

title: 503. Which of the following is a Critically Endangered bird of India?
hint: It is the State Bird of Rajasthan.
summary: The Great Indian Bustard is Critically Endangered.

title: 504. Which marine mammal is India's National Aquatic Animal?
hint: It lives in the Ganga river.
summary: The Gangetic Dolphin is India's National Aquatic Animal.

title: 505. Which big cat is found only in the Gir Forest of India?
hint: It is not a tiger.
summary: The Asiatic Lion survives in Gir National Park.

title: 506. Which reptile is known for its long, narrow snout and is Critically Endangered?
hint: It is mainly found in river ecosystems.
summary: The Gharial is a Critically Endangered crocodilian.

title: 507. The Red Panda is mainly found in which region of India?
hint: Look towards the Eastern Himalayas.
summary: Red Pandas inhabit Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh and nearby Himalayan forests.

title: 508. Which convention regulates international trade in endangered species?
hint: Its abbreviation starts with the letter 'C'.
summary: CITES regulates international trade in threatened wildlife and plants.

title: 509. Which species is protected under Project Tiger?
hint: It is India's national animal.
summary: Project Tiger was launched in 1973 for Bengal Tiger conservation.

title: 510. Which of the following animals is commonly called the 'Sea Cow'?
hint: It is a marine herbivore found in the Gulf of Mannar.
summary: The Dugong is popularly known as the Sea Cow.

title: 511. Which endangered mammal is the only ape found in India?
hint: It is found mainly in the forests of Northeast India.
summary: The Western Hoolock Gibbon is India's only ape and is classified as Endangered.

title: 512. Which Critically Endangered bird is endemic to the Eastern Ghats of Andhra Pradesh?
hint: It is one of India's rarest birds and was rediscovered after being thought extinct.
summary: Jerdon's Courser is a Critically Endangered bird endemic to Andhra Pradesh.

title: 513. Which endangered species is protected under Project Elephant?
hint: It is the largest land mammal in Asia.
summary: Project Elephant was launched in 1992 to conserve Asian Elephants.

title: 514. The Nilgiri Tahr is primarily found in which mountain range?
hint: This mountain range runs parallel to India's western coast.
summary: Nilgiri Tahr is endemic to the Western Ghats.

title: 515. Which animal is the mascot of Project Snow Leopard?
hint: It inhabits the Himalayan alpine region.
summary: Project Snow Leopard focuses on conserving the Snow Leopard and its habitat.

title: 516. Which endangered mammal is known for having a long armored body covered with scales?
hint: It curls into a ball when threatened.
summary: The Indian Pangolin is one of the world's most trafficked mammals.

title: 517. Which critically endangered mammal is the world's smallest wild pig?
hint: It is mainly found in Assam.
summary: The Pygmy Hog is endemic to the grasslands of Assam.

title: 518. Which vulture species is Critically Endangered due to the veterinary drug Diclofenac?
hint: It was once the most common scavenger in India.
summary: White-rumped Vulture populations crashed because of Diclofenac poisoning.

title: 519. Which schedule of the Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972 provides the highest level of protection to animals?
hint: Most iconic endangered mammals are listed here.
summary: Schedule I provides absolute protection with the highest penalties.

title: 520. Which endangered marine reptile is famous for its mass nesting (Arribada) on the Odisha coast?
hint: Every year thousands of females come ashore together.
summary: Olive Ridley Turtles are known for synchronized mass nesting called Arribada.

title: 521. Which endangered deer is found only in the Kashmir Valley and is also known as the Kashmir Stag?
hint: Its local name begins with 'H'.
summary: The Hangul (Kashmir Stag) is an Endangered species found mainly in Dachigam National Park, Jammu & Kashmir.

title: 522. Which endangered primate is endemic to the Western Ghats of India?
hint: It has a distinctive silver-white mane around its face.
summary: The Lion-tailed Macaque is an Endangered species endemic to the Western Ghats biodiversity hotspot.

title: 523. Which endangered mammal is the only surviving species of the family Dugongidae?
hint: It is commonly called the 'Sea Cow'.
summary: The Dugong is an Endangered marine mammal found in the Gulf of Mannar, Palk Bay and Andaman & Nicobar waters.

title: 524. Which critically endangered vulture has suffered a drastic population decline mainly due to Diclofenac poisoning?
hint: Its name refers to the color of its lower back.
summary: The White-rumped Vulture is Critically Endangered due to poisoning from veterinary Diclofenac.

title: 525. Which endangered species is the official mascot of the Government of India's 'Project Dolphin'?
hint: It is India's National Aquatic Animal.
summary: Project Dolphin primarily focuses on conserving the Gangetic River Dolphin and its habitat.

title: 526. Which critically endangered bird is endemic to the grasslands of Assam and parts of Nepal?
hint: Its second name refers to a flowering plant.
summary: The Bengal Florican is a Critically Endangered bustard species found in tall grasslands.

title: 527. Which endangered freshwater mammal is found in rivers of South and Southeast Asia and is known for using echolocation?
hint: It is nearly blind.
summary: The Gangetic River Dolphin relies on echolocation to navigate and hunt in muddy river waters.

title: 528. Which critically endangered species is often called the 'umbrella species' of the Himalayan alpine ecosystem?
hint: It is famous for its long tail and elusive nature.
summary: The Snow Leopard is a flagship and umbrella species for Himalayan conservation.

title: 529. Which endangered species is the world's largest living turtle?
hint: Unlike most sea turtles, its shell is leathery rather than hard.
summary: The Leatherback Sea Turtle is the world's largest living turtle species and is classified as Vulnerable globally, with some regional populations being Critically Endangered.

title: 530. Which of the following is NOT an IUCN Red List threat category?
hint: One option refers to a type of protected area rather than a conservation status.
summary: National Park is a protected area designation, not an IUCN Red List category.

title: 531. Which mammal is known as the 'Sea Cow'?
hint: Lives in shallow coastal waters.
summary: Dugong is an endangered marine mammal.

title: 532. Which bird is called the 'flagship species' of Indian grasslands?
hint: State bird of Rajasthan.
summary: Great Indian Bustard is Critically Endangered.

title: 533. Which endangered mammal is found only in Gir Forest?
hint: It is not a tiger.
summary: Gir is the last wild home of this species.

title: 534. Which species is protected under Project Snow Leopard?
hint: Lives above the tree line.
summary: Project launched in Himalayan states.

title: 535. Which reptile has the longest snout among crocodilians?
hint: Found in Chambal River.
summary: Gharial is Critically Endangered.

title: 536. Which Indian state has the largest population of one-horned rhinoceroses?
hint: Kaziranga is located here.
summary: Most Indian rhinos live in Assam.

title: 537. Which endangered deer is called Kashmir Stag?
hint: Found in Dachigam.
summary: Hangul is endemic to Kashmir.

title: 538. Which bird declined rapidly due to Diclofenac poisoning?
hint: A scavenger bird.
summary: Vulture populations crashed after Diclofenac use.

title: 539. Which mammal is India's National Aquatic Animal?
hint: Lives in the Ganga river.
summary: Gangetic Dolphin is India's National Aquatic Animal.

title: 540. Which endangered primate is endemic to the Western Ghats?
hint: It has a silver-white mane.
summary: Lion-tailed Macaque is endemic to the Western Ghats.

title: 541. Which endangered species is known as the 'State Animal of Sikkim'?
hint: It is a small tree-dwelling mammal.
summary: Red Panda is the State Animal of Sikkim.

title: 542. Which bird is India's only endemic ape's closest companion in Northeast forests?
hint: Think of India's only ape first.
summary: Western Hoolock Gibbon inhabits Northeast India.

title: 543. Which endangered reptile is famous for mass nesting at Gahirmatha?
hint: Known for Arribada.
summary: Olive Ridley turtles nest in Odisha.

title: 544. Which endangered species is protected under Project Cheetah?
hint: Reintroduced to Kuno.
summary: African Cheetahs were introduced under Project Cheetah.

title: 545. Which endangered animal is called the 'Mountain Ghost'?
hint: Lives in the Himalayas.
summary: Snow Leopard is nicknamed Mountain Ghost.

title: 546. Which endangered mammal is famous for its armored scales?
hint: Most trafficked mammal.
summary: Indian Pangolin is heavily poached.

title: 547. Which organization maintains the IUCN Red List?
hint: International Union...
summary: IUCN publishes the Red List.

title: 548. Which critically endangered bird is found mainly in Rajasthan and Gujarat?
hint: Largest bustard in India.
summary: Great Indian Bustard survives in arid grasslands.

title: 549. Which protected area is best known for Hangul conservation?
hint: Located near Srinagar.
summary: Dachigam National Park protects Hangul.

title: 550. Which law provides the highest protection to India's endangered wildlife?
hint: Enacted in 1972.
summary: Wildlife (Protection) Act is India's key wildlife law.
